description: Authors state that increasing measurement error reduces the ratio r (fractional Bell fidelity improvement) from distillation
value: Measurement error rate m vs Fractional Bell fidelity improvement r = Fa/Fb
notes: Negative correlation: higher measurement error reduces distillation benefit
term_b: Fractional Bell fidelity improvement r = Fa/Fb

description: Parity lifetime depends strongly on readout resonator frequency; higher frequency resonators require fewer photons for pair-breaking and thus cause QP generation at lower photon numbers
value: Readout resonator frequency ωr/2π ∈ {46, 34, 25, 20} GHz vs Charge-parity lifetime Tg and Te vs photon number n̄
notes: Higher resonator frequency causes onset of QP generation at lower photon numbers; the number of photons required for pair-breaking equals ⌈2Δ/ℏωr⌉
term_b: Charge-parity lifetime Tg and Te vs photon number n̄

description: Authors state that larger gap difference delta_Delta yields lower burst detection rate
value: Gap difference delta_Delta/h vs Burst detection rate (min^-1)
notes: Negative correlation: big-delta device (8.2 GHz) has 0.57 min^-1 vs small-delta device (0 GHz) at 2.76 min^-1, factor ~5 reduction

description: Authors state that the ratio IIP3/P1dB varies by an order of magnitude within the amplifier bandwidth, linked to nonuniform IIP3 across bandwidth
value: IIP3 as function of frequency vs P1dB compression power as function of frequency
notes: IIP3 variation is much smaller than P1dB variation across bandwidth, so their ratio varies by order of magnitude; positive correlation between frequency position in band and compression power
term_b: P1dB compression power as function of frequency
